content_markdown: What is the correct definition of gravitational field strength?

options.label: B
options.text: The weight of a small test mass

options.label: C
options.text: The force acting on a small test mass

options.label: D
options.text: The force per unit mass acting on a small test mass

vibe_explanation: Gravitational field strength $g$ is defined as the gravitational force $F$ exerted per unit mass $m$ on a small point mass placed at that point ($g = \frac{F}{m}$). Option D captures this 'per unit mass' requirement perfectly.
